No Poverty.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to health or well-being.

Quality Education.reason: As a Master of Laws program, the course directly provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and equips students with specialized legal skills for employment (Target 4.4).

Gender Equality.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Merger and Acquisition law is central to economic productivity (Target 8.2), enterprise growth (Target 8.3), and access to financial services (Target 8.10), fostering overall economic growth.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Mergers and acquisitions are key mechanisms for industrial restructuring, innovation, and enterprise access to financial services (Targets 9.2, 9.3), supporting industrial development.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course on M&A law inherently involves the regulation and monitoring of financial markets and institutions (Target 10.5), which can contribute to reducing inequalities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to sustainable cities or communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: M&A law, as part of corporate governance, can indirectly encourage companies to adopt sustainable practices and integrate sustainability information (Target 12.6).

Climate Action.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to climate action.

Life Below Water.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to marine life or oceans.

Life on Land.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: As a law course, it directly promotes the rule of law (Target 16.3) and contributes to building effective, accountable, and transparent economic institutions (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Mergers and acquisitions involve various forms of partnerships. The legal framework studied contributes to policy coherence and macroeconomic stability (Target 17.13, 17.14).
